memoryArtifacts.headline: Eilean Donan Castle, Donan, Isle of Skye, Scotland
memoryArtifacts.description:
History
The name Eilean Donan, or island of Donan, is most probably called after the 6th century Irish Saint, Bishop Donan who came to Scotland around 580 AD. There are several churches dedicated to Donan in the area and it is likely that he formed a small cell or community on the island during the late 7th century.

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Brief Life History of Isabel
When Lady Isabel MacAulay, of Lochbroom was born in 1344, in Kintail, Ross-shire, Scotland, her father, Duncan MacAulay of Lochbroom, was 45 and her mother, Katherine Murchinson, was 31. She married Murdoch Dubh MacKenzie, 4th of Kintail before 1363, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1370, in Scotland, at the age of 26, and was buried in Scotland.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel MacAulay of Lochbroom於1344年出生在蘇格蘭洛-爾金泰爾時，她的父親Duncan MacAulay of Lochbroom45歲，母親Katherine Murchison31歲。她於1363年之前與Murdoch Dubh Mackenzie 4th Lord of Kintail在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1370年在蘇格蘭享年26歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: MacAulay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (mainly Ross and Cromarty and Inverness):
from Gaelic Mac Amhalghaidh ‘son of Amhalghadh’, an Old Gaelic personal name of unexplained etymology. Compare the Irish form McAuley , also Manx Kewley , Cowley , and Callow .
from Gaelic Mac Amhlaoibh ‘son of Amhlao(i)bh’, Gaelic form of the Old Norse personal name Óláfr (see Olafsen ). Compare McAuliffe .

variants.name: McAulay
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Macaulay  . Compare the Irish form   McAuley  .

variants.name: McCauley
meanings: Scottish (Dumbartonshire) and Irish (Westmeath): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Amhalghaidh ‘son of Amhalghadh’, a personal name of uncertain origin. The best known family of Mac Amhalghaidh is from Westmeath, and there were families called Mac Amhlaoibh in counties Cork and Fermanagh (a branch of the Maguires), as well as a family in southwest Scotland which has migrated to Ulster. Compare the Scottish form Macaulay .

Scottish (Hebrides) and Irish (Fermanagh): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Amhlaoibh or Mac Amhlaidh ‘son of Olaf’, from Gaelic forms of the Old Norse personal name Áleifr, Óláfr (see Olafsen ). Compare McAuliffe .

variants.name: McAuley
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Declan.
Irish: variant of   McCauley  . Compare the Scottish form   Macaulay  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

She married Murdoch Dubh Mackenzie 4th Lord of Kintail before 1363, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel MacAulay of Lochbroom est née en 1344, à Kintail, Ross-shire, Écosse, son père, Duncan MacAulay of Lochbroom, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Katherine Murchison, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Murdoch Dubh Mackenzie 4th Lord of Kintail avant 1363, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1370, à Écosse, à l’âge de 26 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel MacAulay of Lochbroom(이)가 1344년 스컷랜드 로스-실르 킨테일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Duncan MacAulay of Lochbroom(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Murchison(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1363년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Murdoch Dubh Mackenzie 4th Lord of Kintail(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1370년 스컷랜드에서 26세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabel MacAulay of Lochbroom родилась в 1344 г. в местности кинтеил, росс-шир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Duncan MacAulay of Lochbroom было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Murchison, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Murdoch Dubh Mackenzie 4th Lord of Kintail перед 1363 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1370 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 26 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.
